accept: docs/act-evaluation.md exists and addresses the four high-level questions concretely.
Analysis is updated after each substantial alpha-trial in another repo, with what worked / what didn't / which concerns sharpened or changed.
If a comparison to beads/doit's commit pattern materially shifts the architecture recommendation, that change is reflected.
payload.description:
Andrew's framing 2026-05-10: the project-level question isn't 'are issues closed' but 'is this a more efficient and effective way to manage tasks in agent-driven projects than Claude's default markdown approach? Useful alternative to something with rapidly scaling surface area like beads? Easy/intuitive for agents to pick up? Well architected, scalable, well-structured for this part of agent-based engineering?'

Initial analysis lives at docs/act-evaluation.md (committed in the same session this issue was filed). This issue is the placeholder for keeping that analysis updated as we learn more from real-project use.
payload.title: evaluate act vs alternatives — analysis doc for adoption decisions
